nameMeanings.name: Batchelder
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk): altered form of Batchelor , showing the folk-etymology influence of the word elder, with which it is not in fact connected.

variants.name: Batchelor
meanings: English: status name for a young knight or novice at arms, Middle English and Old French bacheler (medieval Latin baccalarius). The word had already been extended to mean ‘(young) unmarried man’ by the 14th century, but it is unlikely that many bearers of the surname derive from the word in that sense.

History: The Reverend Stephen Bachiler (c. 1561–1656) was a Puritan nonconformist, born in Hampshire, England, who came to New England in 1632, at the age of 71. In 1638/9 he was the leader of the founders of Hampton, NH.

variants.name: Bachelder
meanings: Altered form of English   Batchelder  . Compare   Bacheller  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrix Batchelder was born about 1380, in Oxfordshire, England, her father, John Bachelor, was 3 and her mother, Harriet, was 11. She married Thomas de Doddington before 1400. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1429, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrix Batchelder(이)가 대략 1380년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bachelor(은)는 3세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Harriet(은)는 11세였다. 그녀는 1400년 이전 Thomas de Doddington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.
